description_long: This is a tall, slender terracotta vase with a single curved handle extending from the neck to the shoulder. The body features intricate black-figure artwork, likely inspired by ancient Greek pottery, depicting human and possibly mythological figures in a narrative scene. The neck is elongated and flares outward at the rim, while the base is wide and stable, providing support for the vessel. The surface shows signs of age, with some faded paint and worn areas, especially on the handle and the back. The coloration is primarily a natural reddish-brown clay with black painted details and decorative bands. The overall craftsmanship and ornamentation suggest it is a decorative piece, suitable for display in a public or cultural urban setting.
description: A tall, slender terracotta vase with a single handle and black-figure artwork, featuring reddish-brown clay and black painted motifs. The surface shows signs of age and wear, enhancing its antique character.
description_view_0: Front view showing the detailed black-figure artwork and the full handle attached to the neck and shoulder.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curvature of the neck and the continuation of the painted motifs, with less visibility of the handle.
description_view_2: Rear three-quarter view displaying the handle's attachment and more worn, undecorated clay surface.
description_view_3: Back view focusing on the plain, unadorned surface and the full curve of the handle.
